1.description: (Color online) How Do EJR and S&P Evaluate Rated Firms? Notes. This figure presents excerpts from the samples of credit rating reports produced by EJR and S&P. The excerpts show how the two CRAs evaluate rated firms’ quality. In panel (a), the screenshot shows the valuation results done by EJR for Adobe Inc. in June 2021. The relative valuation results are derived based on three multiples (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization; net income; and revenues) under three scenarios (base, optimistic, and pessimistic). The numbers in bold font show the specific multiples in each scenario and the corresponding predicted share price. Average share prices over the last three years are shown in the last column. In panel (b), the screenshot shows how S&P adjusts key financial variables reported by Coca-Cola HBC AG. The adjusted numbers are then used to compute interest- or debt-coverage ratios.

2.description: (Color online) Comparison of Rating Update Frequency Between EJR and S&P Notes. This figure presents the cumulative percentage of rating updates within different time intervals for EJR and S&P. The x -axis is the number of months between the current rating update and the previous rating update for a given firm. The y -axis shows the cumulative percentage of rating updates along the different time intervals for EJR and S&P. Rating updates that happen 24 months or more after the previous update are grouped into 24.
